heading: Conservation of private grazing land
label: 3839bb Conservation of private grazing land

children.text: establishing a coordinated and cooperative Federal, State, and local grazing conservation program for management of private grazing land;
strengthening technical, educational, and related assistance programs that provide assistance to owners and managers of private grazing land;
conserving and improving wildlife habitat on private grazing land;
conserving and improving fish habitat and aquatic systems through grazing land conservation treatment;
protecting and improving water quality;
improving the dependability and consistency of water supplies;
identifying and managing weed, noxious weed, and brush encroachment problems on private grazing land; and
integrating conservation planning and management decisions by owners and managers of private grazing land, on a voluntary basis.

children.heading: Department
children.label: 1 Department
children.text: The term “Department” means the Department of Agriculture.

children.heading: Private grazing land
children.label: 2 Private grazing land
children.text: The term “private grazing land” means private, State-owned, tribally-owned, and any other non-federally owned rangeland, pastureland, grazed forest land, and hay land.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 3 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Private grazing land conservation assistance
children.label: c Private grazing land conservation assistance

children.heading: Assistance to grazing landowners and others
children.label: 1 Assistance to grazing landowners and others
A
children.text: maintaining and improving private grazing land and the multiple values and uses that depend on private grazing land;

children.label: B
children.text: implementing grazing land management technologies;

children.label: i
children.text: planning, managing, and treating private grazing land resources;

children.label: ii
children.text: ensuring the long-term sustainability of private grazing land resources;

children.label: iii
children.text: harvesting, processing, and marketing private grazing land resources; and

children.label: iv
children.text: identifying and managing weed, noxious weed, and brush encroachment problems;

children.label: D
children.text: protecting and improving the quality and quantity of water yields from private grazing land;

children.label: E
children.text: maintaining and improving wildlife and fish habitat on private grazing land;

children.label: F
children.text: enhancing recreational opportunities on private grazing land;

children.label: G
children.text: maintaining and improving the aesthetic character of private grazing land;

children.label: H
children.text: identifying the opportunities and encouraging the diversification of private grazing land enterprises; and

children.label: I
children.text: encouraging the use of sustainable grazing systems, such as year-round, rotational, or managed grazing.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: A Funding
children.text: If funding is provided to carry out this section, it shall be provided through a specific line-item in the annual appropriations for the Natural Resources Conservation Service.

children.heading: Technical assistance and education
children.label: B Technical assistance and education
children.text: Personnel of the Department trained in pasture and range management shall be made available under the program to deliver and coordinate technical assistance and education to owners and managers of private grazing land, at the request of the owners and managers.

children.label: i
children.text: land-grant colleges and universities (as defined in ); andsection 3103 of title 7

children.label: ii
children.text: nongovernmental organizations.

children.label: A
children.text: there is a severe lack of technical assistance for farmers and ranchers that graze livestock;

children.label: B
children.text: Federal budgetary constraints preclude any significant expansion, and may force a reduction of, current levels of technical support; and

children.label: C
children.text: farmers and ranchers have a history of cooperatively working together to address common needs in the promotion of their products and in the drainage of wet areas through drainage districts.

children.heading: Establishment of grazing demonstration
children.label: 2 Establishment of grazing demonstration
children.text: In accordance with paragraph (3), the Secretary may establish 2 grazing management demonstration districts at the recommendation of the grazing land conservation initiative steering committee.

children.heading: Proposal
children.label: A Proposal
children.text: Within a reasonable time after the submission of a request of an organization of farmers or ranchers engaged in grazing, the Secretary shall propose that a grazing management district be established.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: B Funding
children.text: The terms and conditions of the funding and operation of the grazing management district shall be proposed by the producers.

children.label: i
children.text: is reasonable;

children.label: ii
children.text: will promote sound grazing practices; and

children.label: iii
children.text: contains provisions similar to the provisions contained in the beef promotion and research order issued under in effect on .section 2903 of title 7April 4, 1996

children.heading: Area included
children.label: D Area included
children.text: The area proposed to be included in a grazing management district shall be determined by the Secretary on the basis of an application by farmers or ranchers.

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: E Authorization
children.text: The Secretary may use authority under the Agricultural Adjustment Act ( et seq.), reenacted with amendments by the Agricultural Marketing Agreement Act of 1937, to operate, on a demonstration basis, a grazing management district.7 U.S.C. 601

children.heading: Activities
children.label: F Activities
children.text: The activities of a grazing management district shall be scientifically sound activities, as determined by the Secretary in consultation with a technical advisory committee composed of ranchers, farmers, and technical experts.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: e Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $60,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2002 through 2023.
